fieldsToExtract.extractionGuidance: Look for Total Revenue from operations
Calculate this by taking Total profit before tax and adding back Finance costs and Total depreciation, depletion and amortisation expense
Profit After Tax
Net Cash Flows
Total Net Worth
Look in Total non-current liabilities for Long term borrowings
only if not explicitly mentioned, use 'Short term borrowings' as a proxy for CPLTD
Calculated as Current Assets - Current Liabilities
Sum of Long term borrowings and Short term borrowings
Total assets
Calculated as (EBITDA / Total revenue from operations) * 100
Calculated as (PAT / Total revenue from operations) * 100
Calculated as (Total interest bearing debt / TNW)
Calculated as (Total interest bearing debt / EBITDA)
Divide Total current assets by Total current liabilities
Calculated as (EBIT / Finance costs)

if not explicitly found, then calculate EBIT (Profit Before Tax + Finance Costs) and divide by total debt service (Finance Costs + Short term borrowings from balance sheet as proxy for principal repayments), noting the principal repayment is an approximation
Calculated as (Trade receivables current / Total revenue from operations) * 365
Calculated as (Trade payables current / (Cost of materials consumed + Purchases of stock-in-trade)) * 365

Calculated as (Inventories / Cost of Goods Sold) * 365. Cost of goods sold can be derived from COGS derived from 'Cost of materials consumed' + 'Purchases of stock-in-trade' + 'Changes in inventories of finished goods, work-in-progress and stock-in-trade'.
